title: Agents frequently die at low health levels
insight: Low-HP deaths (health < 30) account for 92% of all deaths, indicating that agents often fail due to insufficient health.
evidence: 92% of deaths occur when the agent's health is below 30.

title: Agents rarely succeed and frequently die in short episodes
insight: The mean episode length for successes (deaths) is significantly shorter than that for failures, suggesting a high failure rate.
evidence: Mean episode length for deaths: 101 steps vs Mean episode length for successes: 0 steps.

title: Agents often die near the beginning of episodes
insight: Given the low-HP death frequency and short success episode lengths, agents may be dying early in their attempts.
evidence: Low-HP deaths account for 92% of all deaths.
